name: For Other People
title: Du'as for Other People: what to say to thank, bless, and love others for Allah's sake, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: Authentic du'as to say to and for other people: thanking with jazak Allahu khayran, blessing a host, loving for Allah's sake, and what to say when praised.
keywords: jazakallahu khayran, dua to thank someone, may Allah reward you, love for the sake of Allah, dua when praised, barakallahu fik, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Faith is not only what passes between you and Allah in private. It also shows in how you answer the person in front of you: the one who did you a kindness, the one who told you they love you for Allah's sake, the one who prayed for you, the one who praised you to your face. The sunnah gives you words for each of these too.

These are the adhkar of good companionship. Small phrases that turn an ordinary exchange into worship: a thank-you that becomes a du'a, a blessing returned, love met with love. Learn them and you carry a little of the Prophet's ﷺ courtesy into every room.
intro: Each of these belongs to a moment with another person: when someone does you good, when they pray for you, when they say they love you for Allah, when a loan is repaid, when you are praised, and when you praise someone else. Reach for the one that fits the exchange.

They are short by design, meant to be said out loud and in the moment. Use the Arabic where you can and the transliteration while you learn, and let the meaning shape how you treat the person before you.
closing: Notice how often the reward in these du'as is simply handed back: someone blesses you, and you ask Allah to bless them; someone loves you for His sake, and you ask that He love them. Good is kept moving between believers, and Allah is named as its source every time. This is what it means to be easy, grateful company.
O Allah, make us a mercy to those around us, quick to thank, quick to bless, and quick to love for Your sake alone.

faq.q: What do I say to thank someone?
faq.a: Say Jazak Allahu khayran, may Allah reward you with good. The Prophet ﷺ taught that whoever says this to the one who did him a kindness has given the fullest praise. It is a du'a, not just a thank-you, and it returns the favour to the One who can truly repay it.

faq.q: What do I reply when someone says they love me for the sake of Allah?
faq.a: Reply Ahabbakal-ladhi ahbabtani lah, may He for whose sake you love me, love you. The Prophet ﷺ taught us to meet love for Allah's sake with a du'a that the same love be answered by Allah Himself.

faq.q: What should I say when someone praises me?
faq.a: Turn to Allah with a du'a: O Allah, do not take me to account for what they say, forgive me for what they do not know, and make me better than they imagine. Praise can swell the soul, and this keeps the heart honest before Allah.

faq.q: How do I respond if I am the one praising someone?
faq.a: The Prophet ﷺ warned against absolute praise. Say what you genuinely see as good, and add that Allah is the one who truly knows them and that you do not declare anyone pure before Him. It keeps praise truthful and free of flattery.

email.subject: What to say when someone does you good
email.preview: Short du'as to say to and for other people: thanking, blessing, and loving for Allah's sake.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Faith is not only what passes between you and Allah in private. It also shows in how you answer the person in front of you: the one who did you a kindness, the one who told you they love you for Allah's sake, the one who prayed for you, the one who praised you to your face. The sunnah gives you words for each of these.

We have gathered the adhkar of good companionship: the thank-you that becomes a du'a (Jazak Allahu khayran), the blessing you return when someone blesses you, what to say when someone loves you for Allah's sake, the words for a repaid loan, and how to answer when you are praised, or when you are the one offering praise. Each comes with its Arabic, a transliteration, the meaning, and its source.

Notice how often the reward is simply handed back: someone blesses you, and you ask Allah to bless them; someone loves you for His sake, and you ask that He love them. Good is kept moving between believers, with Allah named as its source every time.

The full collection, formatted to read or recite, is here:

social.hook:
Someone does you a kindness.

The Prophet ﷺ taught a four-word reply that turns your thank-you into a du'a.
social.caption:
The du'as of good companionship, what to say to and for the people around you.

How to thank someone so your gratitude becomes a prayer (Jazak Allahu khayran), how to return a blessing, what to reply when someone says they love you for Allah's sake, and how to keep your heart honest when you are praised.

Notice the pattern: good is kept moving between believers, and Allah is named as its source every time.

Save this, and read the full collection with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/social

#JazakAllahuKhayran #LoveForTheSakeOfAllah #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

197.title: Returning a greeting or kindness: "And you"
197.note: A simple reply that turns a kind word back toward the one who offered it.

198.title: Thanking someone: "May Allah reward you with good"
198.note: Jazak Allahu khayran. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever says this to the one who did him good has given him the fullest praise, handing the reward to the only One who can truly repay it.

199.title: Protection from the Dajjal through Surat al-Kahf
199.note: Whoever memorises ten verses from the beginning of Surat al-Kahf is protected from the False Messiah, and seeks refuge in Allah from his trial after the final tashahhud in every prayer.

200.title: When someone says "I love you for Allah's sake"
200.note: Ahabbakal-ladhi ahbabtani lah. You meet love for Allah's sake with a du'a that the same love be answered by Allah Himself.

201.title: Blessing the one who feeds or hosts you
201.note: Asking Allah to bless your host in their family and their wealth, a du'a the Prophet ﷺ would make for those who served him.

202.title: When a loan is repaid
202.note: A blessing for the one who lent to you: the reward for a loan is heartfelt praise and returning what was borrowed.

203.title: Seeking refuge from hidden shirk
203.note: A guard for the heart in the company of others: asking refuge from associating anything with Allah knowingly, and forgiveness for the shirk one may not even notice.

204.title: Replying to a blessing: "And may Allah bless you"
204.note: Wa fika barakallah. When someone says barakallahu fik, you return the blessing to them in kind.

205.title: When you fear a bad omen
205.note: Affirming that no portent and no good exist except from Allah, and none is worthy of worship but Him. The Prophet ﷺ, by contrast, was pleased by a good word and would take it as a hopeful sign.

231.title: When you must praise someone to their face
231.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that if you must praise a companion, say only what you genuinely see and add that Allah is his true assessor, for you cannot declare anyone pure before Allah.

232.title: When you are the one being praised
232.note: Turn the praise into a du'a: ask Allah not to hold you to account for what they say, to forgive what they do not know, and to make you better than they imagine.
